authoritative_sources.title: EIA Survey Forms
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EIA's official survey-forms page is authoritative for which electric-power forms and instructions govern plant, generator, balancing-area, generation, fuel, and emissions-related records.

authoritative_sources.title: A Guide to EIA Electric Power Data
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EIA's official electric-power data guide identifies the public data families and form relationships behind generator characteristics, generation, fuel, stocks, emissions, and balancing-area records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric source definition and lineage mapping

authoritative_sources.title: Generator Availability Data System (GADS)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC's official GADS page is authoritative for the generator classes, ownership expectations, and data-reporting instruction framework behind outage and availability analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, outage evidence handling, and reliability benchmarking

authoritative_sources.title: The GADS Process
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC's GADS process training is a recognized operational workflow reference for how outage events, edits, submissions, and validations move through the generator-availability reporting process.

authoritative_sources.title: IEEE 762-2023 Standard Definitions for Use in Reporting Electric Generating Unit Reliability, Availability, and Productivity
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IEEE 762 is the recognized power-industry standard for reliability, availability, and productivity terminology and indexes, making it authoritative for metric-definition discipline and cross-system comparability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric calculation and benchmarking boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Part 75 Policy and Technical Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's Part 75 resource hub is the official baseline for emissions monitoring policy, QA, and recordkeeping controls governing power-plant emissions evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: emissions evidence validation and exception review

authoritative_sources.title: ECMPS Reporting Instructions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's ECMPS reporting instructions are the official workflow reference for preparing hourly emissions, monitoring plan, and QA submissions used in power-sector reporting and audit trails.

authoritative_sources.title: FERC Form No. 1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC Form No. 1 is the official regulatory reporting form for annual electric utility data and constrains how operating and financial metrics can be organized for regulated reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Commission Guidance on Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC Release 33-10751 is authoritative for KPI and metric disclosure discipline, including definitions, methodology disclosure, estimates, and changes in calculation relevant to external reporting.

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm fleet scope, unit identifiers, period, systems of record, source precedence, and refresh posture before analysis starts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: metric calculation and source hierarchy selection
workflow_stages.description: Define the metric, denominator, interval, exclusions, and mapping between plant, market, outage, and environmental records before any trend or variance output is assembled.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: cross-system reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile historian, settlement, GADS, EIA, ECMPS, and ERP or BI outputs without collapsing conflicts or silently replacing authoritative records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: analysis or reporting package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the report, metric dictionary, narrative summary, confidence posture, thresholds, and traceability notes for the intended internal or external audience.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Review whether the output crosses into dispatch, engineering, environmental certification, market settlement, or public disclosure authority and escalate rather than overstate.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: historian, EMS, SCADA, and meter extracts
GADS outage event logs, derate records, and outage plans
generation, fuel, stock, and fuel-receipt records
ECMPS hourly emissions files, monitoring plans, and QA artifacts
market settlement statements and dispatch-interval reports
tenant metric dictionaries, disclosure calendars, and signoff matrices
fleet KPI decks, reconciliation notes, and caveat or escalation packages
systems_of_record: asset registry or plant master system
EPA ECMPS or CEMS data management tools
market settlement and dispatch reporting systems

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source hierarchy required for the analysis
prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytical briefs, reconciliation notes, caveat notes, and threshold summaries without overstating authority
escalate: dispatch decisions, unit commitment, or return-to-service approval
engineering root-cause approval or maintenance-scope decisions
environmental compliance certification or formal emissions filing approval
market-settlement approval, legal interpretation, or external disclosure signoff
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or metric definitions are unresolved
bypass outage, emissions, or market controls to produce a cleaner metric
approve operations, engineering, or compliance decisions because trends appear favorable
state unsupported causal, benchmark, readiness, or disclosure claims

domain_failure_modes: gross and net generation or nameplate and seasonal capability are mixed without a definition note and create false trends
historian, settlement, GADS, and EIA records are collapsed into a single KPI without preserving source lineage
availability or outage metrics reuse thermal logic across variable generation or storage assets without explicit mapping
emissions analytics imply compliance certainty despite open Part 75 monitoring exceptions or QA issues
fleet performance narratives imply dispatch readiness or return-to-service approval without operator or engineering authority
external or investor-style claims are made without methodology, signoff chain, or disclosure caveats
tenant-specific overrides silently replace the public baseline and produce metric drift

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Availability and productivity formulas differ across thermal, hydro, wind, solar, and storage portfolios
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenant metric dictionaries and asset-class mappings must be supplied at runtime when cross-technology aggregation is requested.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: ISO and RTO market interval, settlement, and performance definitions vary materially by region
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Market-region manuals or tenant policy must be supplied whenever settlement or dispatch-interval context changes the requested metric.

notes: The package distinguishes analytical authority from dispatch, engineering, compliance-certification, and disclosure authority.
Public-source baselines constrain metric definitions and evidence handling, while tenant-specific mappings remain required for safe execution.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package.
